question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 45.00% on 2026-03-14 for the Metaculus question "Will the DOJ officially remove the requirement for U.S. attorneys to consult with the Public Integrity Section before prosecuting lawmakers before November 4, 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP on Q38209 will exceed 45% on March 14, 2026. The CP has been trending down from 47% in late January to a stable 43% over the past 2+ weeks. It has briefly touched 45% several times but always reverts to 43%. The underlying question (DOJ removing the 9-85.110 consultation requirement for prosecuting lawmakers) has seen no formal policy change despite 10 months of consideration — section 9-85.210 was suspended but 9-85.110 remains in effect. With 122 forecasters providing stability, no imminent news catalyst, and only 12 days until resolution, the CP is most likely to remain at or below 45%.

factors.description: CP currently at 43%, needs to exceed 45% — a 2+ percentage point gap to cross
Persistent downward trend: CP fell from 47% in late Jan to stable 43% over past 2+ weeks
CP has briefly touched 45% multiple times but always reverts to 43%, suggesting resistance at threshold
122 forecasters makes the CP relatively stable and resistant to large single-forecaster swings
Underlying policy (9-85.110) remains formally in effect despite 10 months of 'considering' removal — no imminent catalyst for CP increase
Possible upward push: DOJ could announce formal removal of 9-85.110 in the next 12 days, pushing CP above 45%
12-day window is short, limiting opportunity for major CP movement without a news catalyst

reasoning:
**Forecast: 21% probability (NO is more likely)**

Key reasoning:
- **CP is at 43%, below the 45% threshold** — the most recent data point shows the CP needs to move up 2+ points
- **Downward trend** — CP fell from 47% → 43% over the past month and has stabilized at 43%
- **Resistance at 45%** — CP has touched 45% multiple times but always reverts back to 43%
- **No imminent catalyst** — The underlying policy (section 9-85.110 requiring PIN consultation before prosecuting lawmakers) remains formally in effect. Only section 9-85.210 (campaign financing) was suspended. The DOJ has been "considering" removal for 10 months without acting
- **Short window + stable base** — 122 forecasters and 12 days make a large upward shift unlikely without a major news catalyst
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the DOJ officially remove the requirement for U.S. attorneys to consult with the Public Integrity Section before prosecuting lawmakers before November 4, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/38209)
